children.heading: Nonhighway uses
children.label: a Nonhighway uses
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (i), if gasoline is used in an off-highway business use, the Secretary shall pay (without interest) to the ultimate purchaser of such gasoline an amount equal to the amount determined by multiplying the number of gallons so used by the rate at which tax was imposed on such gasoline under section 4081. Except as provided in paragraph (2) of subsection (f) of this section, in the case of gasoline used as a fuel in an aircraft, the Secretary shall pay (without interest) to the ultimate purchaser of such gasoline an amount equal to the amount determined by multiplying the number of gallons of gasoline so used by the rate at which tax was imposed on such gasoline under section 4081.

children.heading: Intercity, local, or school buses
children.label: b Intercity, local, or school buses
A
children.text: furnishing (for compensation) passenger land transportation available to the general public, or

children.label: B
children.text: the transportation of students and employees of schools (as defined in the last sentence of section 4221(d)(7)(C)),

children.heading: Limitation in case of nonscheduled intercity or local buses
children.label: 2 Limitation in case of nonscheduled intercity or local buses
children.text: Paragraph (1)(A) shall not apply in respect of gasoline used in any automobile bus while engaged in furnishing transportation which is not scheduled and not along regular routes unless the seating capacity of such bus is at least 20 adults (not including the driver).

children.heading: Exempt purposes
children.label: c Exempt purposes
children.text: If gasoline is sold to any person for any purpose described in paragraph (2), (3), (4), (5), or (6) of section 4221(a), the Secretary shall pay (without interest) to such person an amount equal to the product of the number of gallons of gasoline so sold multiplied by the rate at which tax was imposed on such gasoline by section 4081. The preceding sentence shall apply notwithstanding paragraphs (2) and (3) of subsection (f). Subsection (a) shall not apply to gasoline to which this subsection applies.

children.heading: Time for filing claims; period covered
children.label: d Time for filing claims; period covered

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), not more than one claim may be filed under subsection (a), not more than one claim may be filed under subsection (b), and not more than one claim may be filed under subsection (c), by any person with respect to gasoline used during his taxable year; and no claim shall be allowed under this paragraph with respect to gasoline used during any taxable year unless filed by such person not later than the time prescribed by law for filing a claim for credit or refund of overpayment of income tax for such taxable year. For purposes of this subsection, a person’s taxable year shall be his taxable year for purposes of subtitle A.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: 2 Exception
children.text: For payments per quarter based on aggregate amounts payable under this section and section 6427, see section 6427(i)(2).

children.heading: Application to sales under subsection (c)
children.label: 3 Application to sales under subsection (c)
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, gasoline shall be treated as used for a purpose referred to in subsection (c) when it is sold for such a purpose.

children.heading: Gasoline
children.label: 1 Gasoline
children.text: The term “gasoline” has the meaning given to such term by section 4083(a).

children.label: i
children.text: which (at the time of such use), is registered, or is required to be registered, for highway use under the laws of any State or foreign country, or

children.label: ii
children.text: which, in the case of a highway vehicle owned by the United States, is used on the highway.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this subparagraph, the term “off-highway business use” does not include any use in a motorboat.

children.heading: Fisheries and whaling
children.label: ii Fisheries and whaling
children.text: The term “off-highway business use” shall include any use in a vessel employed in the fisheries or in the whaling business.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The term “off-highway business use” shall include any use in a vehicle which meets the requirements described in clause (ii).

children.label: I
children.text: the design-based test, and

children.label: II
children.text: the use-based test.

children.label: I
children.text: to which there has been permanently mounted (by welding, bolting, riveting, or other means) machinery or equipment to perform a construction, manufacturing, processing, farming, mining, drilling, timbering, or similar operation if the operation of the machinery or equipment is unrelated to transportation on or off the public highways,

children.label: II
children.text: which has been specially designed to serve only as a mobile carriage and mount (and a power source, where applicable) for the particular machinery or equipment involved, whether or not such machinery or equipment is in operation, and

children.label: III
children.text: which, by reason of such special design, could not, without substantial structural modification, be used as a component of a vehicle designed to perform a function of transporting any load other than that particular machinery or equipment or similar machinery or equipment requiring such a specially designed chassis.

children.heading: Use-based test
children.label: iv Use-based test
children.text: For purposes of clause (ii)(II), the use-based test is met if the use of the vehicle on public highways was less than 7,500 miles during the taxpayer’s taxable year. This clause shall be applied without regard to use of the vehicle by any organization which is described in section 501(c) and exempt from tax under section 501(a).

children.heading: Exempt sales; other payments or refunds available
children.label: f Exempt sales; other payments or refunds available

children.heading: Gasoline used on farms
children.label: 1 Gasoline used on farms
children.text: This section shall not apply in respect of gasoline which was (within the meaning of paragraphs (1), (2), and (3) of section 6420(c)) used on a farm for farming purposes.

children.label: A
children.text: in aviation which is not commercial aviation (as defined in section 4083(b)), or

children.label: B
children.text: in commercial aviation (as so defined) with respect to the tax imposed by section 4081 at the Leaking Underground Storage Tank Trust Fund financing rate and, in the case of fuel purchased after , at so much of the rate specified in section 4081(a)(2)(A) as does not exceed 4.3 cents per gallon.September 30, 1995

children.label: A
children.text: the Leaking Underground Storage Tank Trust Fund financing rate under section 4081, and

children.label: B
children.text: so much of the rate specified in section 4081(a)(2)(A) as does not exceed the rate applicable under section 4041(a)(1)(C)(ii).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: All provisions of law, including penalties, applicable in respect to the tax imposed by section 4081 shall, insofar as applicable and not inconsistent with this section, apply in respect of the payments provided for in this section to the same extent as if such payments constituted refunds of overpayments of the tax so imposed.

children.heading: Examination of books and witnesses
children.label: 2 Examination of books and witnesses
children.text: For the purpose of ascertaining the correctness of any claim made under this section, or the correctness of any payment made in respect of any such claim, the Secretary shall have the authority granted by paragraphs (1), (2), and (3) of section 7602(a) (relating to examination of books and witnesses) as if the claimant were the person liable for tax.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: h Regulations
children.text: The Secretary may by regulations prescribe the conditions, not inconsistent with the provisions of this section, under which payments may be made under this section.

children.heading: Income tax credit in lieu of payment
children.label: i Income tax credit in lieu of payment

children.heading: Persons not subject to income tax
children.label: 1 Persons not subject to income tax
A
children.text: the United States or any agency or instrumentality thereof, a State, a political subdivision of a State, or any agency or instrumentality of one or more States or political subdivisions, or

children.label: B
children.text: an organization exempt from tax under section 501(a) (other than an organization required to make a return of the tax imposed under subtitle A for its taxable year).

children.heading: Exception
children.label: 2 Exception
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not apply to a payment of a claim filed under subsection (d)(2).

children.heading: Allowance of credit against income tax
children.label: 3 Allowance of credit against income tax
children.text: For allowance of credit against the tax imposed by subtitle A, see section 34.
For civil penalty for excessive claims under this section, see section 6675.
For fraud penalties, etc., see chapter 75 (section 7201 and following, relating to crimes, other offenses, and forfeitures).
For treatment of an Indian tribal government as a State (and a subdivision of an Indian tribal government as a political subdivision of a State), see section 7871.
